title: Styphelia leptospermoides
taxonomy: Styphelia leptospermoides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Styphelia, which is a group of plants endemic to Australia. The plant is often referred to as a shrub, and its classification is part of the broader understanding of its botanical characteristics. Styphelia leptospermoides is also known to be a member of the myrtle family, Ericaceae, which includes a diverse range of plant species. This classification is essential for understanding the plant's evolutionary history and its place within the plant kingdom.

parts: Styphelia leptospermoides, also known as Tasmanian pepperbush, is a plant species native to Australia. The medicinal parts of Styphelia leptospermoides are its leaves and berries.
